slots.rod: RIVERTEX Fenix 11'5" (Match Rod, 6.5-17.5 Lb, Mod Fast, Level 23)

leader_recommendation.why: Walleye and Sauger are famously line-shy predators. A fluoro leader gives extra stealth near the hook. The medium length lets the Spawn Sack drift naturally below the float in the strike zone.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 7-13 ft. Walleye and Sauger hold deep near bottom structure, especially during daylight. At The Dock of Peace, try 10-12 ft. Go shallower (6-8 ft) at dawn/dusk when they move up to feed.

issues: Spawn Sack is not listed as effective bait for any Emerald Lake fish in the fp-collective database
Hook #6 is too small for Walleye (#4/0 recommended) and Sauger (#5/0 recommended)
Chubby float works but may not have enough buoyancy for the deeper presentations Walleye need
Good rod/reel combo  -  Fenix is well suited for float fishing predators
improvements: Switch bait to Leeches  -  #1 bait for Walleye and effective on Sauger at Emerald Lake
Upsize hook to #2/0 or #4/0  -  Walleye have larger mouths and need bigger hooks
Alternatively switch to Small Minnows  -  covers Walleye, Sauger, Chain Pickerel, and Northern Pike
Keep the Chubby float for daytime, switch to Glowing Pear-Shaped for dawn/dusk/night sessions
This slot has great potential as your primary Walleye/Sauger float rig with the right bait and hook

slots.rod: JigWinner 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-13 Lb, Fast)
issues: Very minor: Light rod may struggle with trophy Chain Pickerel (7.7 lb) during aggressive runs
improvements: EXCELLENT setup  -  the Titanium Leader is a game-changer for Emerald Lake's toothy predators
Pike and Pickerel will bite through fluoro/mono but NOT titanium  -  this protects your lure
Turtle Spoon 1/4 Oz targets Walleye ($86/lb), Sauger ($82/lb), and all pickerel species
Fluoro .012" mainline provides good abrasion resistance against pike teeth above the leader
This is your BEST predator hunting setup  -  actively cast near structure for maximum income

slots.rod: Loki 7'6" SE (Spinning Rod, Heavy, 5/7-2 1/3 Oz, 6-24 Lb, Moderate)
slots.terminal_tackle: Jighead 3/4 Oz. #3/0 with Craw 2 1/2" soft plastic
issues: Heavy rod is overpowered but acceptable for pike/pickerel  -  these are aggressive fighters
3/4 Oz jighead is heavy  -  will work in deep water but may snag on bottom structure
No titanium/wire leader  -  pike and pickerel can bite through mono
Craw soft plastics work for predators but aren't specifically listed for Emerald Lake species
improvements: Add a titanium or wire leader (like slot 2)  -  pike WILL bite through mono .010"
Downsize to 1/2 Oz jighead for better action and fewer snags
Craw pattern is a good instinct  -  crawfish action triggers predator strikes from pike and pickerel
Work the jig near submerged structure  -  hop it along bottom with pauses, pike hit on the fall
This is a solid backup predator slot to complement slot 2's spoon approach

leader_recommendation.why: Light leader for panfish. Maggots attract Pumpkinseed, Black Crappie, and Bluntnose Minnow. The thin fluoro .008" mainline already provides stealth, but a thinner leader at the hook maximizes bites from wary panfish.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-7 ft. Panfish hold at various depths at Emerald Lake. Start at 4-5 ft near weed edges at The Dock of Peace. Yellow Perch may be deeper at 6-7 ft.

issues: Durability getting low (15975/18000)  -  monitor rod condition
Maggots cover Pumpkinseed and Black Crappie well but don't attract Walleye/Sauger
Glowing float is good for dawn/dusk visibility
improvements: Solid panfish setup  -  Maggots + #6 hook is well-matched for Emerald Lake panfish
Deploy on RodPod Trio at The Dock of Peace for passive panfish income
Consider carrying Bloodworms as backup  -  also covers Bluntnose Minnow and Yellow Perch
This rod's low durability means it may break during a session  -  consider repairing soon

slots.rod: Aurora 8'2" Sport (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-1/2 Oz, 3-10 Lb, Fast)
issues: No titanium leader  -  risky when pike or pickerel strike, they can bite through fluoro
Narrow Spoon is excellent for Walleye/Sauger but you already have slot 2 covering these
Overlaps significantly with slot 2's coverage
improvements: Add a titanium leader like slot 2  -  you'll lose spoons to pike without one
Consider swapping lure to target different species  -  a Nano Spoon or Casting Spoon would cover Yellow Perch better
Alternatively, switch to a crankbait or jerkbait to diversify your predator approach
Good rod/reel combo  -  just needs leader protection and lure differentiation from slot 2

slots.rod: ValueSpin 6'3" (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-6.5 Lb, Fast)
issues: No titanium leader  -  pickerel can cut fluoro .008"
Nano Spinner is great for Yellow Perch but the #1/0 hook may miss smaller panfish
3-6.5 Lb line weight is light if a Northern Pike takes the spinner
improvements: Add a short titanium leader for pike/pickerel protection
Nano Spinner's vibration and flash is excellent for Yellow Perch ($34/lb) and pickerel species
The short 6'3" rod is ideal for fishing from the Lupus boat in tight spots
Upgraded fluoro .008" is a big improvement over the old mono  -  much less visible
Good for actively covering water from the boat to locate fish schools

slots.rod: MCT McCarp 13'2" (Match Rod, 9-25.5 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Medium leader for versatile panfish/perch targeting. Crickets are not listed as a bait for Emerald Lake fish, so effectiveness depends on the general attraction. A longer leader helps present bait naturally in deeper water where Yellow Perch hold.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 5-10 ft. Yellow Perch hold at mid-depth. At Midland Charm, try 6-8 ft near structure.

issues: Crickets are NOT listed as effective bait for any Emerald Lake fish species in the database
Hook #6 is good size for panfish but this rod is overpowered for sub-2 lb fish
13'2" rod is very long  -  harder to use from the boat
Fluoro .012" is thicker than needed for panfish
improvements: CRITICAL: Switch bait from Crickets to Maggots or Red Worms  -  Crickets don't appear in Emerald Lake's bait lists
Maggots cover Pumpkinseed, Black Crappie, and Bluntnose Minnow
Alternatively use Bloodworms  -  covers Black Crappie, Yellow Perch, and Bluntnose Minnow
Consider Marshmallows for Black Crappie + Pumpkinseed coverage
Deploy on RodPod Trio alongside slot 4 for double passive panfish income

passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: The dock provides a stable platform for the RodPod Trio with deep water access directly in front. Panfish hold near the dock pilings, and Walleye/Sauger cruise the deeper water at the dock's edge  -  your float rods cover both zones without moving.
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: Cast spinning rods from the dock itself or wade along the shore within 50 ft of your rod stand. Stay close enough to see your floats and hear bite alerts. The deeper water off the dock holds Walleye, Sauger, and Chain Pickerel.
strategy: Set RodPod Trio at The Dock of Peace  -  place all 3 float rods at different depths and angles
Slot 1 (Fenix + Chubby float): Cast to deep water (10-12 ft) targeting Walleye/Sauger  -  SWITCH BAIT TO LEECHES
Slot 4 (OmniFloat + Maggots): Cast to mid-depth (4-6 ft) near dock pilings for panfish
Slot 7 (McCarp + Crickets): Cast to shallow-mid (5-8 ft) along weed edge  -  SWITCH TO MAGGOTS/BLOODWORMS
Actively fish with slot 2 (Turtle Spoon + Titanium Leader) casting from the dock for pike/pickerel/walleye
When a float dips, immediately switch to that rod  -  panfish bites are quick, Walleye bites are slower but still need response within 5-10 seconds
After landing a fish, re-bait and re-cast to the same spot  -  fish congregate in productive areas
Cycle: fish actively for 2-3 casts, check floats, respond to bites, repeat. Sell keepnet when full.
For NIGHT sessions: Switch to Walleye-focused passive setup  -  all 3 stand rods with Leeches at deep settings (10-15 ft). Walleye feed aggressively after dark and will hit passive rigs hard.
income_estimate: Passive float rods (3 rods soaking): ~3-5 fish per rod per in-game hour depending on species and bait
Active spinning (1 rod casting): ~5-10 fish per in-game hour with predator lures
Combined output: ~15-25 fish per in-game hour across all 4 active lines
If focusing on Walleye ($86/lb, avg 3.3 lb): ~$285 per Walleye x 3-5 passive catches/hr = $855-$1,425/hr from stand alone
Plus active spinning income from pike/pickerel: ~$500-$1,000/hr additional
Estimated total: $1,300-$2,400 per in-game hour with optimized passive + active setup
Night Walleye sessions can push income even higher  -  Walleye bite rate doubles after dark

notes: Garry Scott Lupus Motor Boat  -  massive advantage at Emerald Lake! Access mid-lake structure for Walleye, Sauger, and Pike
Luminox Headlamp  -  enables effective night fishing. Walleye and Sauger are most active at dawn/dusk/night
RodPod Trio  -  deploy 3 float rods from shore or dock for passive income while boating
FishStronghold M Plus Keepnet  -  upgraded storage for larger catches
Outlander Pro Vest  -  improved carry capacity over Wild Element Deluxe
Glasses Classic  -  reduces glare on water for spotting fish and structure
Groundbait is not critical for Emerald Lake  -  most target fish are predators caught on lures/live bait
Could help concentrate panfish at dock spots if pursuing Pumpkinseed or Black Crappie

boat.license_required: Advanced New York License (required for boat + night fishing)

recommendation.features: Motor boat with sonar capability. Far superior to kayaks for covering water and finding predator fish.
recommendation.why: You already have the Lupus equipped! This is a huge advantage at Emerald Lake. Motor to mid-lake structure, use the sonar to find drop-offs and weed edges where Walleye, Sauger, and Pike hold. Cast slots 2, 3, 5, 6 from the boat while float rods soak on shore.

fishing_tips: Use the Lupus to motor to deep structure  -  Walleye and Sauger hold at 10-15 ft near drop-offs
Cast slot 2 (Turtle Spoon + Titanium Leader) near submerged points and weed edges from the boat
Night fishing from the boat with the Luminox Headlamp is devastating for Walleye  -  they feed aggressively after dark
Set up RodPod Trio at The Dock of Peace with slots 1, 4, 7 before heading out on the boat
Return to dock periodically to check float rods and sell accumulated panfish
Chain Pickerel and Northern Pike ambush near shallow weed lines  -  motor along edges and cast into pockets
Use the sonar to locate schools of Yellow Perch in open water  -  cast Nano Spinner through them
boat.cost_analysis: Advanced NY License needed for boat: 1-day $800, 3-day $2,280, week $5,040, unlimited 30 BC. Travel $2,500 + extend $500/day. The Lupus boat gives access to the highest-value fish (Walleye $86/lb, Sauger $82/lb) in deep water  -  easily pays for the license cost.

missing_setups: Common Shiner and Golden Shiner need Dough Balls or Semolina Balls  -  neither bait is currently equipped
Slot 1's Spawn Sack is not listed for any Emerald Lake fish  -  needs bait change for effective use
Slot 7's Crickets are not listed for any Emerald Lake fish  -  needs bait change
No titanium leaders on slots 3, 5, 6  -  risk of losing gear to pike/pickerel bite-offs
xp_optimization: Catch ALL 12 species for first-catch XP bonuses  -  you're currently missing Common Shiner and Golden Shiner
Unique Chain Pickerel (7.7 lb max) and Unique Sauger (6.9 lb max) give massive first-catch bonuses
Trophy Walleye and Chain Pickerel give 2-3x XP  -  use slot 2 with titanium leader for best trophy chance
Fish both spots (The Dock of Peace and Midland Charm) for location discovery XP
Night fishing with headlamp gives access to Walleye's peak activity  -  huge XP potential
Use the Lupus boat to reach mid-lake spots that most anglers can't access  -  less fishing pressure = more bites
income_optimization: Walleye at $86/lb (max 5.7 lb) is the top total earner  -  up to $496 per trophy. Target with slot 2 from the boat
Sauger at $82/lb (max 6.9 lb) has the best $/fish potential  -  up to $567 per trophy. Target with slots 2, 5
Northern Pike at $77/lb (max 4.9 lb)  -  solid earner, target with slot 3 jig near weed edges
Chain Pickerel at $68/lb (max 7.7 lb)  -  heaviest fish here, up to $525 per trophy
Fill FishStronghold keepnet with Walleye and Sauger before selling  -  these are the money fish
Emerald Lake is cheaper than Falcon Lake ($2,500 travel vs $4,000) with comparable income potential
Night fishing for Walleye is the most profitable strategy  -  they're most active and less wary after dark
priority_changes: CRITICAL: Slot 1  -  switch Spawn Sack to Leeches or Small Minnows and upsize hook to #2/0-#4/0 for Walleye/Sauger
CRITICAL: Slot 7  -  switch Crickets to Maggots or Bloodworms  -  Crickets don't work at Emerald Lake
IMPORTANT: Add titanium leaders to slots 3, 5, and 6  -  pike and pickerel WILL bite through your line
Slot 5 overlaps with slot 2  -  consider diversifying lure type (crankbait or jerkbait) for different predator approach
Bring Dough Balls or Semolina Balls if you want to complete all 12 species (Common + Golden Shiner)
Buy an Advanced NY License for boat + night fishing  -  the combination of Lupus boat and Walleye night fishing is extremely profitable
